figures.caption: Figure 8: Causal diagram of the MR-CCC structural model. The causal effect of ligand expression on pathway activity is βX+βXZZi, allowing receptor-modulated communication. Genetic instruments Gi and Hi identify ligand and receptor expression respectively; observed covariates Vi and unmeasured confounders Ui may affect all three observed biological variables (Xi, Zi, Yi). Solid arrows indicate causal effects; dashed arrows indicate the unobserved confounder pathways that MR blocks.

Figure 4: Estimates of the receptor-modulated interaction effect βXZ across scenarios, sample sizes, and methods. Boxplots of the estimated ligand–receptor interaction effect β̂XZ across 20 replicates, stratified by scenario and sample size. MR-BMA and MVMR are omitted as neither models the interaction term βXZ . The red dashed line indicates the true value (0 for S1 and S3; 0.3 for S2). Under S1 and S3, where the interaction is truly absent, MR-CCC remains well-calibrated near zero.

Table 3: Simulation results under the main-effect-only scenario (S3). Scenario 3 (γ = 1, βX = 0.3, βXZ = 0). Each entry is reported as mean (sd) across 20 replicates. Score denotes the communication score produced by each method; higher values and higher rejection rates are preferred when signal is present. “—” indicates that βXZ is not estimated by MR-BMA or MVMR.

Figure 3: Estimates of the ligand main effect βX across scenarios, sample sizes, and methods. Boxplots of the estimated ligand main effect β̂X across 20 replicates, stratified by scenario and sample size. Rows correspond to scenarios S1–S3; columns correspond to sample size. For MR-BMA the plotted value is the model-averaged causal effect (MACE) for the ligand exposure. The red dashed line indicates the true value of βX in each scenario (0 for S1; 0.3 for S2 and S3).

Figure 5: Posterior inclusion probabilities for the NK cells→monocytes analysis. Posterior inclusion probabilities for all 41 ligand–receptor–pathway triplets in the NK cells → Monocytes analysis (n = 651 donors), ranked from highest to lowest PIP. Points are coloured by pathway class. Black rings identify the eight discoveries with PIP > 0.5: the two IL18 receptor-chain pairs lead at PIP=0.98 and 0.93, followed by PTGES3–PTGER4 (0.91), SLC6A6–GABBR1 (0.86), IL1B–IL1RAP (0.72), and three additional interferon and prostaglandin pairs (0.56–0.61). The dashed vertical line and shaded region mark the PIP > 0.5 discovery zone.

Table 2: Simulation results under the main + interaction signal scenario (S2). Scenario 2 (γ = 1, βX = 0.3, βXZ = 0.3). Each entry is reported as mean (sd) across 20 replicates. Score denotes the communication score produced by each method; higher values and higher rejection rates are preferred when signal is present. “—” indicates that βXZ is not estimated by MR-BMA or MVMR.

Figure 6: Pathway-specific posterior mean effects for the NK cells → Monocytes analysis. The left panel shows |β̂(s) X |, the absolute standardized ligand main effect, and the right panel shows |β̂(s) XZ |, the absolute standardized receptor-modulation effect. Point size encodes effect magnitude; color encodes PIP on a 0–1 scale, with black borders marking the eight discoveries (PIP > 0.5). Discoveries concentrate in the GABA, Interferon, Interleukin, and Prostaglandin pathway classes. The two IL18 receptor-chain pairs (IL18RAP, IL18R1 ) are visible as the largest, darkest points in the Interleukin column of the right panel, reflecting their opposing and large interaction magnitudes (|β̂(s) XZ | = 3.19 and 2.13, respectively). ICAM adhesion pairs show uniformly small, pale points in both panels.

Figure 2: Communication scores across scenarios, sample sizes, and methods. Boxplots of communication scores across 20 replicates, stratified by scenario (rows: S1–S3) and sample size (columns: n ∈ {500, 1000, 10,000, 30,000}). For MR-CCC the score is the posterior inclusion probability Pr(γ = 1 | data). For MR-BMA the score is MIPX . For OLS the score is 1− pF , where pF is the joint F -test p-value for H0 : βX = βXZ = 0. For MVMR the score is 1− pt, where pt is the t-test p-value for H0 : βX = 0. The red dashed line marks the true communication state (0 for S1; 1 for S2 and S3). Higher scores are preferred when signal is present; lower scores are preferred under the null.

Figure 7: Receptor-modulated ligand effect curves for the eight NK cells→monocytes discoveries. Receptor-modulated ligand effects for the NK cells → Monocytes analysis (n = 651 donors). Each curve represents β̂ (s) X + β̂ (s) XZ · (Z/sd(Z)), plotted over the observed donor range of standardized receptor expression. Only the eight discovery pairs (PIP > 0.5) are displayed, grouped into the four pathway panels with confirmed discoveries (GABA, Interferon, Interleukin, and Prostaglandin). The Interleukin panel shows the most striking pattern: the IL18–IL18R1 curve (upward slope; β̂ (s) XZ = +2.13) and the IL18– IL18RAP curve (steep downward slope; β̂ (s) XZ = −3.19) diverge in opposite directions, revealing opposing receptor-chain modulation for the same cytokine. In the Prostaglandin panel, PTGES3–PTGER4 (strong negative slope) and PTGES3–PTGER2 (near-horizontal) illustrate receptor-subtype-specific modulation. In the GABA panel, SLC6A6–GABBR1 crosses zero near the population mean. In the Interferon panel, both IFNG receptor-chain curves (IFNGR1 and IFNGR2 ) decline from positive intercepts with sign-reversals in the upper tercile of the receptor distribution.

Figure 1: Overview of the MR-CCC framework, simulation benchmark, and lead biological discovery. (1) Ligand–receptor co-expression between a sender and a receiver cell can be driven by an unmeasured confounder U , so correlation alone cannot establish causal communication. (2) MR-CCC uses cis-eQTLs G and H as instruments for ligand X and receptor Z and adjusts for covariates V , blocking U . The working outcome model identifies the receptor-modulated ligand effect ∂Y/∂X = βX +βXZZ; a spike–and–slab prior on (βX , βXZ) yields a posterior inclusion probability PIP = Pr(γ = 1 | data), with discovery declared at PIP > 0.5. (3) Application to NK cells→monocytes in the OneK1K cohort (n = 651 donors): eight discoveries across four pathway classes, led by an IL-18 receptorchain dissociation (IL18R1 : β̂ (s) XZ = +2.13; IL18RAP : β̂ (s) XZ = −3.19). (4) Simulation at n = 500 across S1 (null), S2 (main + interaction), and S3 (main only): MR-CCC controls the null score, remains unbiased for βX under confounding, and is the only method that recovers a confounding-corrected βXZ .
